At a Glance Total Time 1 hour and 10 minutes Number of Questions 60 Percent of Total Score 66.67% Writing Instrument Pencil required Electronic Device None allowed Instructions Section I of this exam contains 60 multiple-choice questions. Fill in only the circles for numbers | through 60 on your answer sheet. Indicate all of your answers to the multiple-choice questions on the answer sheet. No credit will be given for anything written in this exam booklet, but you may use the booklet for notes or scratch work. After you have decided which of the suggested answers is best, completely fill in the corresponding circle on the answer sheet. Give only one answer to each question. Your total score on the multiple-choice section is based only on the number of questions

MACROECONOMICS

Section I

Time—1 hour and 10 minutes

60 Questions

Directions: Each of the questions or incomplete statements below is followed by five suggested answers or
completions. Select the one that is best in each case and then fill in the corresponding circle on the answer sheet.

text: Which of the following typically occurs during an expansionary phase of a business cycle?

options.A: Nominal interest rates decrease.
options.C: The price level decreases.
options.D: Government transfer payments increase.
options.E:
Employment increases.

. Economic growth refers to an increase in which of

the following?

(A) Government spending

(B) Consumption spending

(C) Nominal gross domestic product

(D) Potential real gross domestic product
(E) Household wealth

. If both the nominal interest rate and the expected

inflation rate increase, what will happen to the
real interest rate?

(A) It will increase because the expected inflation
rate has increased.

(B) It will increase because the nominal interest
rate has increased.

(C) It will increase if the expected inflation
rate increases by more than the nominal
interest rate.

(D) It will decrease because the nominal interest
rate has increased.

(E) It will decrease if the expected inflation
rate increases by more than the nominal
interest rate.

. Which of the following statements is true about an

expansionary fiscal policy?

(A) It decreases demand for loanable funds.

(B) It decreases the equilibrium price level.

(C) It decreases the equilibrium real interest rate.
(D) It increases aggregate demand.

(E) It increases the money supply.

Unauthorized copying or reuse of
any part of this page is illegal.

options.A: Government spending
options.C: Nominal gross domestic product
options.D: Potential real gross domestic product
options.E:
Household wealth

(D) It will decrease because the nominal interest rate has increased. (E) It will decrease if the expected inflation rate increases by more than the nominal interest rate. . options.A: It decreases demand for loanable funds.

options.C: It decreases the equilibrium real interest rate.
options.D: It increases aggregate demand.
options.E:

text: Which of the following will most likely result from deflation?

options.A: Increased nominal interest rates
options.C: Increased real value of fixed incomes
options.D: Decreased purchasing power of cash
options.E:
Decreased real wealth

. Which of the following is a defining characteristic

of a fractional reserve banking system?

(A) The existence of a central bank with
a monopoly on money creation

(B) The use of paper money backed by
a commodity such as gold or silver

(C) The fact that banks retain an amount of bank
reserves that is less than the amount of
customer demand deposits

(D) The requirement that banks maintain a certain
percentage of their reserves as a deposit in
an account at the central bank

(E) The regulations that separate investment
banking from commercial banking

text: Which of the following will most likely result from deflation? (A) Increased nominal interest rates (B) Increased business profits (C) Increased real value of fixed incomes (D) Decreased purchasing power of cash (E) Decreased real wealth . Which of the following is a defining characteristic of a fractional reserve banking system?

options.A:
The existence of a central bank with
a monopoly on money creation
options.B:
The use of paper money backed by
a commodity such as gold or silver
options.C:
The fact that banks retain an amount of bank
reserves that is less than the amount of
customer demand deposits
options.D:
The requirement that banks maintain a certain
percentage of their reserves as a deposit in
an account at the central bank
options.E:
The regulations that separate investment
banking from commercial banking

text: A negative aggregate supply shock will result in which of the following in the short run?

options.A:
An increase in the price level and a decrease
in the unemployment rate
options.B:
A decrease in the price level and an increase
in the unemployment rate
options.C:
A decrease in both the price level and real
output
options.D:
An increase in both the price level and real
output
options.E:
An increase in both the price level and the
unemployment rate

text: Which of the following combinations of fiscal and monetary policies will correct a severe recession?

options.A:
Increasing income tax rates and increasing
administered interest rates
options.B:
Increasing income tax rates and decreasing
administered interest rates
options.C:
Decreasing income tax rates and increasing
administered interest rates
options.D:
Decreasing income tax rates and decreasing
administered interest rates
options.E:
Decreasing income tax rates and increasing
the policy rate

. Which of the following would decrease the

United States net exports to South Korea?

(A) South Korean computer companies sell more
computers to the United States.

(B) South Korean insurance companies provide
fewer services to the United States.

(C) South Koreans spend more vacations in the
United States.

(D) United States banks provide more banking
services to South Korea.

(E) United States smartphone companies sell
more smartphones to South Korea.

An increase in the purchases of newly constructed
houses will result in which of the following?

(A) Aggregate demand will decrease as a result of
a decrease in the price level.

(B) Aggregate demand will increase as a result of
an increase in investment spending.

(C) Aggregate demand will increase as a result of
an increase in exports.

(D) Aggregate demand will not change, since
consumer spending has not changed.

(E) Aggregate demand will not change, since
investment spending has not changed.

any part of this page is illegal.

text: Suppose that a country’s nominal gross domestic product (GDP) was $1,000 in year 1 and $2,000 in year 2. If year | is the base year and real GDP in year 2 was $1,000, which of the following is true?

options.A: Prices fell by 50% between year 1 and year 2.
options.B: Prices doubled between year 1 and year 2.
options.C:
Prices remained the same between year | and
year 2.
options.D:
More goods and services were produced in
year 2 than in year 1.
options.E:
Fewer goods and services were produced in
year 2 than in year 1.

Assume a country has limited reserves in its
banking system. To decrease the money supply,
the country’s central bank can do which of the
following?

(A) Sell government bonds

(B) Decrease the discount rate

(C) Decrease the required reserve ratio
(D) Increase taxes

(E) Increase government spending

Which of the following types of unemployment is
caused by a recession?

(A) Hidden
(B) Frictional
(C) Seasonal
(D) Structural
(E) Cyclical

If the United States government increases deficit
spending, which of the following will occur as a
result of the change in the interest rate?

(A) The United States dollar will appreciate in
foreign exchange markets.

(B) Household savings in the United States will
decrease.

(C) The United States exports will increase.

(D) The demand for United States dollars will
decrease.

(E) Private investment in plant and equipment in
the United States will increase.

If real interest rates in the United States fall
relative to real interest rates in Great Britain,
which of the following will occur?

(A) British investors will buy more United States
securities.

(B) British exports to the United States will
increase.

(C) The supply of dollars will decrease.

(D) United States investors’ demand for British
pounds will decrease.

(E) The British pound will appreciate relative to
the dollar.

of income will

(C) increase by $150
(D) increase by $750
(E) increase by $1,250

Assume that Country X and Country Y are trading
partners. If the average income in Country X
increases, which of the following will occur in the
foreign exchange market?

(A) The demand for Country X’s currency will
increase, and Country X’s currency will
appreciate.

(B) The demand for Country Y’s currency will
increase, and Country X’s currency will
depreciate.

(C) The demand for Country Y’s currency will
increase, and Country X’s currency will
appreciate.

(D) The supply of Country X’s currency will
decrease, and Country X’s currency will
appreciate.

(E) The supply of Country Y’s currency will
increase, and Country Y’s currency will
depreciate.

text: Assuming a banking system with limited reserves, which of the following is a monetary policy aimed at increasing the equilibrium interest rate in the money market?

options.A: Raising taxes
options.C: Lowering the federal funds rate
options.D: Selling bonds on the open market
options.E:
Lowering the required reserve ratio

Which of the following monetary and fiscal
policy mixes will reduce unemployment?

(A) Decreasing interest on reserves and
increasing taxes

(B) Decreasing interest on reserves and
decreasing taxes

(C) Increasing interest on reserves and
increasing government spending

(D) Increasing interest on reserves and
decreasing government spending

(E) Increasing interest on reserves and
increasing taxes

circular flow model of an economy?

(A) Taxes received from the public equal
government spending.

(B) Imports equal exports.

(C) Total spending equals total income.

(D) Consumption plus saving equals investment.

(E) Saving plus investment equals imports plus
exports.

sloping, which of the following will cause
inflation?

(A) An increase in long-run aggregate supply
(B) An increase in short-run aggregate supply
(C) An increase in aggregate demand
(D) A decrease in aggregate demand
(E) A decrease in aggregate demand and

an increase in aggregate supply

text: Sam and Bill run a leaf raking and lawn mowing business. In one day, Sam can rake 3 lawns or mow 5 lawns whereas Bill can rake 2 lawns or mow 6 lawns. Which of the following correctly describes Sam’s and Bill’s absolute and comparative advantages?

options.A:
Sam has a comparative advantage in raking
and an absolute advantage in mowing.
options.B:
Sam has a comparative advantage in mowing
and an absolute advantage in raking.
options.C:
Sam has a comparative advantage in mowing
and an absolute advantage in mowing.
options.D:
Bill has a comparative advantage in mowing
and an absolute advantage in mowing.
options.E:
Bill has a comparative advantage in raking
and an absolute advantage in mowing.

People who have given up looking for jobs are
classified as

(A) cyclically unemployed
(B) frictionally unemployed
(C) structurally unemployed
(D) discouraged workers
(E) underemployed workers

Banks expand the money supply when

(A) issuing credit cards
(B) printing money
(C) cashing checks
(D) making loans

(E) accepting deposits

The economy is currently in long-run equilibrium.
If the central bank increases the money supply, in
the long run the price level will

(A) increase, and output will remain at the
full-employment level

(B) increase, and output will be above the
full-employment level

(C) increase, and output will be below the
full-employment level

(D) remain unchanged, and output will remain
at the full-employment level

(E) remain unchanged, and output will be above
the full-employment level

When the United States dollar appreciates against
the euro, which of the following will most likely
happen?

(A) European firms will pay fewer euros
for equipment purchased from the
United States.

(B) European products will become more
expensive for United States consumers.

(C) United States tourists will pay fewer dollars
for trips to Europe.

(D) The European trade deficit will increase.

(E) The United States trade deficit will decrease.

Which of the following will occur in the money
market when the aggregate price level increases?

(A) The money supply will increase and nominal
interest rates will decrease.

(B) The demand for money will increase and
nominal interest rates will decrease.

(C) The demand for money will increase and
nominal interest rates will increase.

(D) The demand for money will decrease and
nominal interest rates will decrease.

(E) The opportunity cost of holding money will
decrease.

any part of this page is illegal.

A bank has $200 million in demand deposits
and $150 million in reserves. The reserve ratio
is 20 percent. What is the maximum amount of

(B) $150 million
(C) $110 million

Which of the following is true about the
Phillips curve?

(A) A change in aggregate demand does not shift
the long-run Phillips curve (LRPC).

(B) A change in aggregate demand does not
cause a movement along the short-run
Phillips curve (SRPC).

(C) The LRPC shows the trade-off between
unemployment and inflation but the SRPC
does not.

(D) Changes in expected inflation affect the
LRPC only.

(E) Negative supply shocks affect the LRPC
only.

(C) The LRPC shows the trade-off between
unemployment and inflation but the SRPC
does not.

(D) Changes in expected inflation affect the
LRPC only.

(E) Negative supply shocks affect the LRPC
only.

text: Which of the following is most likely included in gross domestic product? (A) Matt gives his secondhand bicycle to his brother. (B) Sal paints his own bicycle. (C) Ali buys a new bicycle. (D) Mike buys a share of stock in a bicycle firm. (E) Daniel bikes to school every day. Which of the following is likely to result in an inflow of financial capital to Country R? (A) A decrease in Country R’s government budget deficit An increase in personal income tax rates in Country R (B) An increase in administered interest rates by the central bank of Country R (C) (D) (E) An increase in Country R’s trade surplus Increased political instability in Country R A bank has $200 million in demand deposits and $150 million in reserves. The reserve ratio is 20 percent. What is the maximum amount of loans the bank can make from its reserves? (A) $750 million (B) $150 million (C) $110 million (D) $50 million (E) $40 million Which of the following is true about the Phillips curve?

options.A:
A change in aggregate demand does not shift
the long-run Phillips curve (LRPC).
options.B:
A change in aggregate demand does not
cause a movement along the short-run
Phillips curve (SRPC).
options.C:
The LRPC shows the trade-off between
unemployment and inflation but the SRPC
does not.
options.D:
Changes in expected inflation affect the
LRPC only.
options.E:
Negative supply shocks affect the LRPC
only.

text: When the total amount the government spends equals tax revenues in any given year, which of the following must remain constant?

options.A: The real interest rate
options.C: Real gross domestic product
options.D: The price level
options.E: The money supply

text: How does the automatic adjustment mechanism move the economy to potential real gross domestic product (GDP) in the long run when current real GDP is above potential GDP?

options.A:
Nominal wages fall, shifting the short-run

aggregate supply curve to the right.
options.B:
Nominal wages fall, shifting the short-run

aggregate supply curve to the left.
options.C:
Nominal wages do not change, shifting

the short-run aggregate supply curve to

the right.
options.D:
Nominal wages rise, shifting the short-run

aggregate supply curve to the right.
options.E:
Nominal wages rise, shifting the short-run
aggregate supply curve to the left.

text: Which of the following statements about inflation is true?

options.A:
The expected inflation rate is the difference
between nominal and real interest rates.
options.B:
Low expected inflation rates lead to high
inflation rates.
options.C: Lenders lose from expected inflation.
options.D: Lenders gain from unexpected inflation.
options.E: Workers lose from expected inflation.

text: The opportunity cost of an activity is

options.A: the amount of time spent on the activity
options.B:
the value of the benefit received from
performing the activity
options.C:
the value of the forgone benefit of the next
best alternative
options.D: zero if the activity offered no benefits
options.E:
the sum of benefits from all the sacrificed
alternatives

text: Increased spending on which of the following contributes most to long-term economic growth?

options.A: Social security and other transfer payments
options.C: Education and infrastructure
options.D: Imported consumer goods
options.E: Interest payments on national debt

text: If consumers in Canada increase their demand for products that are manufactured in India, which of the following will occur?

options.A: The Indian rupee will depreciate.
options.C: India’s financial capital inflow will increase.
options.D:
Canada’s financial capital inflow will
increase.
options.E:
The supply of Canadian dollars will decrease.
